correctAnswerItalian: C'è una sezione sulle famiglie nobili.
correctAnswerEnglish: There is a section on noble families.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Descrivere una parte specifica del museo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, parla dell'area dedicata all'aristocrazia locale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Describing a specific part of the museum.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it speaks of the area dedicated to local aristocracy.

english: The column is Roman.
choicesEnglish: The column is Roman.
The statue is Greek.
The door is French.
The street is German.
correctAnswerEnglish: The column is Roman.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Identificare l'origine antica di un reperto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, specifica l'epoca dell'elemento architettonico.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Identifying the ancient origin of an artifact.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it specifies the era of the architectural element.

english: The base dates back to the Middle Ages.
choicesEnglish: The base dates back to the Middle Ages.
The top dates back to the Renaissance.
The end dates back to the present.
The start dates back to the future.
correctAnswerEnglish: The base dates back to the Middle Ages.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Precisare l'epoca di una parte del monumento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, parla della parte inferiore e del suo periodo storico.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Specifying the era of a part of the monument.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it speaks of the lower part and its historical period.

english: There is a festival every year.
choicesEnglish: There is a festival every year.
There is a war every century.
There is a break every hour.
There is a dinner every evening.
correctAnswerEnglish: There is a festival every year.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Informare sulla regolarità di una celebrazione locale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica la cadenza annuale dell'evento.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Informing about the regularity of a local celebration.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the annual frequency of the event.

english: We should go to the festival.
choicesEnglish: We should go to the festival.
We should leave the church.
We should run towards the bar.
We should sleep on the bench.
correctAnswerEnglish: We should go to the festival.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Una proposta di attività sociale e culturale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, suggerisci di partecipare alla celebrazione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A proposal for social and cultural activity.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you suggest participating in the celebration.

english: Yes, let's go together.
choicesEnglish: Yes, let's go together.
No, go alone.
Maybe, we'll see tomorrow.
Always, I love walking.
correctAnswerEnglish: Yes, let's go together.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Accettare la proposta di compagnia.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, confermi che farete la gita in gruppo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Accepting the proposal of company.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you confirm that you will go on the trip as a group.

italian: Fu costruita da una famiglia nobile.
english: It was built by a noble family.
choicesItalian: Fu costruita da una famiglia nobile.
Fu distrutta da un temporale brutto.
Fu venduta da un mercante ricco.
Fu regalata da un re generoso.
choicesEnglish: It was built by a noble family.
It was destroyed by a bad storm.
It was sold by a wealthy merchant.
It was given as a gift by a generous king.
correctAnswerItalian: Fu costruita da una famiglia nobile.
correctAnswerEnglish: It was built by a noble family.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Precisare i committenti storici dell'opera.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, parla della realizzazione voluta dall'aristocrazia.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Specifying the historical patrons of the work.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it speaks of the construction commissioned by the aristocracy.

english: It is not always open.
choicesEnglish: It is not always open.
It is closed forever.
It is free for everyone.
It is dirty outside here.
correctAnswerEnglish: It is not always open.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un avvertimento sugli orari limitati.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che l'accessibilità non è garantita ogni giorno.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A warning about limited hours.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates that accessibility is not guaranteed every day.

english: I can show you the inside.
choicesEnglish: I can show you the inside.
I can call my friend.
I can pay the bill.
I can close the door.
correctAnswerEnglish: I can show you the inside.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'offerta di visita da parte di chi ha le chiavi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti stai proponendo come accompagnatore dentro l'edificio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! An offer of a visit from those who have the keys.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are offering to accompany someone inside the building.

english: Here is the key.
choicesEnglish: Here is the key.
Here is the book.
Here is the street.
Here is the festival.
correctAnswerEnglish: Here is the key.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! L'atto di consegnare lo strumento per aprire.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai dando la chiave per entrare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The act of handing over the tool to open.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are giving the key to enter.

english: Look at the frescoes.
choicesEnglish: Look at the frescoes.
Drink the red wine.
Call the black taxi.
Buy the dessert now.
correctAnswerEnglish: Look at the frescoes.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un invito ad osservare le decorazioni pittoriche.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, suggerisci di guardare i dipinti sui muri.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! An invitation to observe the pictorial decorations.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you suggest looking at the paintings on the walls.

english: They are original.
choicesEnglish: They are original.
They are fake now.
They are new today.
They are dirty here.
correctAnswerEnglish: They are original.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Confermare l'autenticità delle opere d'arte.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che non sono copie o riproduzioni.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Confirming the authenticity of the artworks.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates they are not copies or reproductions.

english: How wonderful!
choicesEnglish: How wonderful!
What a bad thing!
What a deadly bore!
What useless haste!
correctAnswerEnglish: How wonderful!

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Esclamazione di stupore e ammirazione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, esprimi entusiasmo per la bellezza vista.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Exclamation of amazement and admiration.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you express enthusiasm for the beauty seen.

english: Thanks again.
choicesEnglish: Thanks again.
You're welcome immediately.
Sorry never.
Bye later.
correctAnswerEnglish: Thanks again.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un ringraziamento finale molto cortese.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai ringraziando una seconda volta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A very polite final thank you.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are thanking a second time.

english: This town is very beautiful.
choicesEnglish: This town is very beautiful.
This village is very dirty.
This city is very expensive.
This place is very empty.
correctAnswerEnglish: This town is very beautiful.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un complimento sincero al luogo che visiti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, esprimi apprezzamento estetico per il centro abitato.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A sincere compliment to the place you visit.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you express aesthetic appreciation for the inhabited center.

english: Excuse me, I was walking / strolling.
choicesEnglish: Excuse me, I was walking / strolling.
Hi, I was running.
Hello, I was eating.
Good morning, I was sleeping.
correctAnswerEnglish: Excuse me, I was walking / strolling.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Spiegare cosa stavi facendo prima di fermarti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, parli del tuo camminare rilassato per le vie.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Explaining what you were doing before stopping.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you speak of your relaxed walking through the streets.

english: It was a small military camp.
choicesEnglish: It was a small military camp.
It was a large public market.
It was a new modern theater.
It was an old closed bar.
correctAnswerEnglish: It was a small military camp.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Informazione sulle origini difensive del luogo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, descrive la funzione originaria della fondazione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Information on the defensive origins of the place.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it describes the original function of the foundation.

english: Mixtures of eras.
choicesEnglish: Mixtures of eras.
Set of colors.
Group of people.
Pile of stones.
correctAnswerEnglish: Mixtures of eras.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Quando stili storici diversi si sovrappongono.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica la presenza di vari periodi storici nello stesso luogo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! When different historical styles overlap.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates the presence of various historical periods in the same place.

italian: Si dice che una principessa sia stata murata viva.
english: It is said that a princess was walled in alive.
choicesItalian: Si dice che una principessa sia stata murata viva.
Si dice che un re sia stato felice sempre.
Si dice che un cane sia stato perso ieri.
Si dice che un bar sia stato aperto ora.
choicesEnglish: It is said that a princess was walled in alive.
It is said that a king was happy always.
It is said that a dog was lost yesterday.
It is said that a bar was opened now.
correctAnswerItalian: Si dice che una principessa sia stata murata viva.
correctAnswerEnglish: It is said that a princess was walled in alive.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Un racconto drammatico tipico del folclore.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, riporta una voce popolare su un evento tragico.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A dramatic tale typical of folklore.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it reports a popular rumor about a tragic event.

english: Enjoy your visit.
choicesEnglish: Enjoy your visit.
Good night.
Have a good trip.
Enjoy your meal.
correctAnswerEnglish: Enjoy your visit.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un augurio cortese per chi inizia un tour.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un saluto per chi va a vedere dei monumenti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A polite wish for those starting a tour.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a greeting for those going to see monuments.
